title: Telangana heatwave intensifies; Khammam sizzles at 45°C - Telangana Today
full_text:
Home |Hyderabad |Telangana Heatwave Intensifies Khammam Sizzles At 45c
Telangana heatwave intensifies; Khammam sizzles at 45°C
Telangana continues to reel under intense heatwave conditions, with Khammam recording the state’s highest temperature at 45°C. Several northern districts also crossed 44°C, while Hyderabad touched 41.3°C, according to IMD data, with severe warm night conditions reported.
Hyderabad: Heatwave conditions across Telangana continued with Khammam, in the last 24-hours recording the State’s highest at 45 degree Celsius, India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad weather data on Thursday said.
Meanwhile, Hakimpet recorded the highest in Hyderabad at 41.3 degrees Celsius.
According to IMD data, a heatwave has formed over northern and eastern districts of Telangana. After Khammam, the next district to record the highest temperature (in degree Celsius) was Ramagundam (44.6), Nizamabad (44.4), and Adilabad at 44.3.
The northern districts, including Adilabad and Nizamabad, experienced severe warm night conditions, with minimum temperatures at 32.7 and 32.3, respectively.
According to IMD weather data, Hyderabad recorded a maximum of 39.5 i.e., Begumpet station, while other weather monitoring stations at Hakimpet recorded 41.3, and Dundigal recorded 41 degrees Celsius.

title: Hyderabad records its hottest temperature of the season - The Times of India
full_text:
HYDERABAD
: The city recorded its highest temperature of the season on Thursday, with several parts of the city crossing the 43 degrees Celsius mark for the first time this summer.
The highest temperature in the city was recorded at 43.4 degrees Celsius in Karwan, followed by Chandrayangutta at 43.2 degrees Celsius and Amberpet at 43 degrees Celsius. Several other areas, including Uppal, Hayathnagar, Secunderabad and Kapra, recorded temperatures above 42.5 degrees Celsius.
Heatwave conditions continued to grip
Telangana, with 20 districts placed under red alert as temperatures remained significantly above normal across the state. According to weather department data, several districts recorded temperatures above 46 degrees Celsius, while heatwave conditions prevailed at 244 locations statewide.
Peddapalli and Suryapet emerged as the hottest districts, recording 46.5 degrees Celsius each. Districts including Bhadradri Kothagudem, Hanumakonda, Jagtial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Karimnagar, Khammam, Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Mulugu, Nalgonda and Rajanna-Sircilla recorded 46.4 degrees Celsius.
Mahabubabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad and Yadadri-Bhuvanagiri registered 46.3 degrees Celsius, while Adilabad and Warangal touched 46.2 degrees Celsius. Siddipet recorded 46.1 degrees Celsius, while Kamareddy and Jangaon registered 45.9 degrees Celsius and 45.5 degrees Celsius respectively.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast that heatwave conditions are likely to persist over the next few days in districts including Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Khammam and Nalgonda.
The department has advised people to avoid prolonged exposure to the sun, stay hydrated and take necessary precautions, especially during afternoon hours.

title: Telangana reels under severe heatwave as temperatures near 47°C - Hyderabad Mail
full_text:
HYDERABAD: Telangana continued to reel under intense heatwave conditions on Thursday, with temperatures nearing 47°C for the second consecutive day across several districts.
According to data released by the Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS), Munagala in Suryapet district recorded the highest temperature of 46.5°C. Mutharam in Manthani division of Peddapalli district also touched 46.5°C.
Several other locations reported temperatures above 46°C. Mandamarri in Mancherial, Dharmapuri in Jagtial, Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Wyra in Khammam, Palwancha in Bhadradri Kothagudem, Venkatapur in Mulugu and Kaleshwaram in Jayashankar Bhupalpally recorded 46.4°C.
Kattangoor in Nalgonda, Kamalapur in Hanamkonda, Gangadhara in Karimnagar and Vemulawada Rural in Rajanna Sircilla also registered 46.4°C.
Bhongir in Yadadri Bhuvanagiri, Mamda in Nirmal, Mortad in Nizamabad and Kuravi in Mahabubabad recorded 46.3°C. Jainad in Adilabad and Nallabelly in Warangal touched 46.2°C.
Akkanpet in Siddipet recorded 46.1°C, while Dongli in Kamareddy registered 45.9°C. Chilpur in Jangaon recorded 45.5°C and Shankarampet in Medak touched 45.1°C. Madgul in Rangareddy district recorded 44.9°C.
Weather observers said peak heatwave conditions are expected to continue across Telangana for the next four to five days, with no immediate relief in sight.

title: Adilabad touches 44.5 degrees heatwave tightens grip on Telangana - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: The intense summer heat continues to batter Telangana, with mercury crossing the critical 44 degrees Celsius mark in the state's northern pockets.
According to official data released by the India Meteorological Department, Hyderabad, in the last 24 hours till 8.30 am on Tuesday, Adilabad recorded the state's highest maximum temperature of 44.5 degrees Celsius, which is 1.8 degrees Celsius above normal.
The IMD, Hyderabad weather data highlights a stark regional divide, with extreme heat in northern districts and heavily humid, oppressive conditions in the central and southern regions.
After Adilabad, Nizamabad trailed closely with 44.2 degrees Celsius, while Medak and Nalgonda clocked peak temperatures of 42.2 and 41.5 degrees Celsius respectively.
Even areas experiencing slightly lower temperatures are facing immense discomfort due to soaring morning relative humidity levels, which peaked at an intense 86 per cent in Hanmakonda and 75 per cent in Bhadrachalam.
Within the Greater Hyderabad region, the Rajendranagar region recorded the highest at 40 degrees Celsius, followed by Begumpet (39.9), while other peripheral stations like Hakimpet, Patancheru, and Hayath Nagar hovered uncomfortably close to the 40 degrees Celsius threshold.
